questions.text: What is the purpose of linking a dispute case to an FI document?
options: To increase financial transparency
To provide a way of tracking its resolution
To enhance customer satisfaction
To reduce the need for manual data entry

questions.text: What must be done as part of the follow-up process for disputes?
options: The disputes should be ignored until they are resolved automatically
The disputes should be archived immediately after documentation
The disputes need to be identified, categorized, documented, archived, followed up on, and rapidly settled
The disputes should only be followed up if they exceed a financial threshold

questions.text: How can final resolution of a dispute be achieved?
options: By ignoring the dispute
Through the creation/matching of a credit memo, payout, etc.
By letting the dispute resolve over time
Through arbitration

questions.text: Which is NOT a common issue with deductions?
options: FIFO method of clearing disputes with receivables
No process in place to follow up/challenge disputes
Clearing and resolution of deductions not done in a timely manner
High visibility of the real reason of deduction

questions.text: What happens when a deduction is fully cleared?
options: The disputed amount becomes zero and the case remains open
The disputed amount increases and requires further review
The disputed amount becomes zero and the case is automatically closed
The dispute escalates to legal action

questions.text: Reason codes are fixed and cannot be changed once assigned to a dispute.
options: True
False

questions.text: Credit notes created for disputes can be linked back for documentation purposes.
options: True
False
